title: A Complete Simulation Study with causalsim
text: Evaluating causal estimators requires knowing answer. causalsim gives making data-generating process explicit: specify structural model, package simulates data , measure well estimator recovers truth specified.

title: A Complete Simulation Study with causalsim
code: library(causalsim)

title: A Complete Simulation Study with causalsim
text: structural model study : W∼N(0,1),∣W∼Bernoulli(logistic(0.5W)),Y=2A+0.5W+ε,ε∼N(0,1) W \sim N(0,1), \quad \mid W \sim \text{Bernoulli}\!\left(\text{logistic}(0.5\,W)\right), \quad Y = 2A + 0.5W + \varepsilon, \quad \varepsilon \sim N(0,1) causalsim_dgp() terms: one standard-normal confounder, constant effect 2, moderate propensity confounding (logistic coefficient 0.5), moderate baseline shift. true ATE exact effect scalar — Monte Carlo approximation needed. function-valued effects, causalsim_dgp() approximates ATE via 10,000 Monte Carlo draws construction time.
code: dgp <- causalsim_dgp(   n             = 500,   n_confounders = 1,   effect        = 2,   propensity    = "moderate",   baseline      = "moderate" ) dgp #> <causalsim_dgp> #>   n            : 500 #>   true ATE     : 2.0000 #>   heterogeneous: FALSE #>   sigma        : 1.00 #>   covariates   : #>     W       normal  [confounder]

title: A Complete Simulation Study with causalsim
text: causalsim_draw() simulates one dataset DGP. columns .tau .p individual causal effect propensity score — diagnostic metadata available real observational data. moderate confounding, treated control groups differ pre-treatment covariate: difference exactly makes naive regression biased. estimator omits W absorb part association Y treatment coefficient.

title: A Complete Simulation Study with causalsim
text: estimator function accepts data frame returned causalsim_draw() returns named numeric vector. ci_lower ci_upper fields optional enable coverage power metrics. Named lists also accepted, following equivalent:
code: # Naive: regresses Y on A only — omits the confounder naive_est <- function(data) {   fit <- lm(Y ~ A, data = data)   est <- coef(fit)[["A"]]   se  <- sqrt(vcov(fit)["A", "A"])   c(estimate = est, ci_lower = est - 1.96 * se, ci_upper = est + 1.96 * se) }  # OLS: adjusts for the observed confounder W ols_est <- function(data) {   fit <- lm(Y ~ A + W, data = data)   est <- coef(fit)[["A"]]   se  <- sqrt(vcov(fit)["A", "A"])   c(estimate = est, ci_lower = est - 1.96 * se, ci_upper = est + 1.96 * se) } ols_est <- function(data) {   fit <- lm(Y ~ A + W, data = data)   ci  <- confint(fit)["A", ]   list(     estimate = coef(fit)["A"],     ci_lower = ci[1],     ci_upper = ci[2]   ) }

title: A Complete Simulation Study with causalsim
text: causalsim_eval() runs reps independent replications returns tidy summary bias, RMSE, coverage, power Monte Carlo standard errors. naive estimator’s bias substantial: treatment positively correlated WW, also raises YY baseline, unadjusted coefficient absorbs part association. OLS eliminates bias conditioning WW. Coverage naive estimator falls well nominal 95% confidence intervals centered wrong value. summary() adds full distribution per-replication estimates, plot() shows histogram: Distribution OLS estimates 300 replications. Solid line: true ATE. Dashed line: mean estimate.
code: eval_naive <- causalsim_eval(dgp, naive_est, reps = 300L, seed = 1L) eval_naive #> <causalsim_eval>  reps: 300  true ATE: 2 #>  #>    metric  value      se #>      bias 0.2353 0.00587 #>      rmse 0.2562 0.00571 #>  coverage 0.3700 0.02787 #>     power 1.0000 0.00000 eval_ols <- causalsim_eval(dgp, ols_est, reps = 300L, seed = 1L) eval_ols #> <causalsim_eval>  reps: 300  true ATE: 2 #>  #>    metric   value      se #>      bias -0.0002 0.00529 #>      rmse  0.0915 0.00338 #>  coverage  0.9433 0.01335 #>     power  1.0000 0.00000 summary(eval_ols) #> <causalsim_eval_summary>  reps: 300  true ATE: 2 #>  #> Estimate distribution: #>   mean  : 1.9998 #>   sd    : 0.0916 #>   median: 2 #>   [p10, p90]: [1.883, 2.1217] #>  #> Metrics: #>    metric   value      se #>      bias -0.0002 0.00529 #>      rmse  0.0915 0.00338 #>  coverage  0.9433 0.01335 #>     power  1.0000 0.00000 plot(eval_ols)

what: Step 5: Vary Sample Size with causalsim_grid()
title: A Complete Simulation Study with causalsim
text: causalsim_grid() evaluates estimator Cartesian product supplied parameter values, returning tidy data frame metrics cell. vary n across four levels track OLS estimator’s precision improves data. RMSE roughly halves nn quadruples — consistent n\sqrt{n}-rate convergence OLS correctly specified model. Bias stays near zero every sample size.

title: A Complete Simulation Study with causalsim
text: Varying propensity preset shows bias scales confounding. causalsim_grid() accepts one estimator time, run separately combine results. Naive bias grows proportionally confounding strength. OLS remains near zero across three levels WW observed included model.
code: conf_levels <- list(propensity = c("low", "moderate", "high"))  grid_naive <- causalsim_grid(dgp, naive_est,                               vary    = conf_levels,                               reps    = 300L,                               metrics = "bias",                               seed    = 1L)  grid_ols   <- causalsim_grid(dgp, ols_est,                               vary    = conf_levels,                               reps    = 300L,                               metrics = "bias",                               seed    = 1L)  comparison <- rbind(   cbind(estimator = "naive", grid_naive$results),   cbind(estimator = "ols",   grid_ols$results) ) comparison <- comparison[order(comparison$propensity, comparison$estimator), ] rownames(comparison) <- NULL comparison #>   estimator propensity metric         value          se #> 1     naive       high   bias  0.4089432973 0.006098827 #> 2       ols       high   bias -0.0001855971 0.005888572 #> 3     naive        low   bias  0.1305019380 0.005990692 #> 4       ols        low   bias  0.0045047637 0.005556133 #> 5     naive   moderate   bias  0.2419929827 0.005443921 #> 6       ols   moderate   bias -0.0008346139 0.005360097

title: A Complete Simulation Study with causalsim

title: causalsim
text: R package defining causal data generating processes known ground truth evaluating estimator performance .

title: causalsim
text: Structural causal model explicit effect, propensity, baseline functions Named covariate roles: confounder, instrument, effect modifier, noise Preset confounding levels ("low", "moderate", "high") custom functions Exact Monte Carlo true ATE computed construction time Flexible estimator interface: named numeric vector, named list, one-row data frame Tidy performance metrics: bias, RMSE, coverage, power Monte Carlo standard errors Grid evaluation Cartesian product DGP parameters Reproducible via seed control every stage

title: causalsim
text: Requires R 4.0 higher. Install GitHub:
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("chaycereed/causalsim")

title: causalsim
text: causalsim_dgp() specifies structural model. Parameters can scalars, preset strings, functions covariate names.
code: library(causalsim)  dgp <- causalsim_dgp(   n             = 500,   n_confounders = 1,   effect        = 2,   propensity    = "moderate",   baseline      = "moderate" ) dgp

title: causalsim
text: causalsim_draw() simulates one dataset DGP. returned data frame includes covariate columns, treatment , outcome Y, individual effect .tau, propensity .p.
code: dat <- causalsim_draw(dgp, seed = 1L) head(dat)

title: causalsim
text: estimator function accepts data frame returns named numeric vector minimum estimate field. ci_lower ci_upper enable coverage power metrics.
code: ols_est <- function(data) {   fit <- lm(Y ~ A + W, data = data)   est <- coef(fit)[["A"]]   se  <- sqrt(vcov(fit)["A", "A"])   c(estimate = est, ci_lower = est - 1.96 * se, ci_upper = est + 1.96 * se) }  result <- causalsim_eval(dgp, ols_est, reps = 200L, seed = 1L) result  summary(result) plot(result)

title: causalsim
text: causalsim_grid() runs evaluator Cartesian product DGP parameters, returning tidy data frame metrics cell.
code: grid_result <- causalsim_grid(   dgp       = dgp,   estimator = ols_est,   vary      = list(n = c(100L, 250L, 500L, 1000L)),   reps      = 200L,   metrics   = c("bias", "rmse"),   seed      = 1L ) grid_result

title: causalsim

what: causalsim: Simulation-Ready Causal Data Generating Processes — causalsim-package
title: causalsim: Simulation-Ready Causal Data Generating Processes — causalsim-package
text: Construct, simulate, evaluate causal data generating processes (DGPs) known ground truth. Variable roles assumption violations first-class concepts. output tidy ggplot2-compatible.

title: causalsim: Simulation-Ready Causal Data Generating Processes — causalsim-package
text: Maintainer: Chayce Reed code.jcr@gmail.com

what: Create a causal data generating process — causalsim_dgp
title: Create a causal data generating process — causalsim_dgp
text: Defines causal DGP known ground truth. Covariates can specified via shorthand count arguments (Option B), explicit named list covar() objects (Option ), combined.

title: Create a causal data generating process — causalsim_dgp
code: causalsim_dgp(   n,   effect = 1,   propensity = "moderate",   baseline = 0,   sigma = 1,   covariates = list(),   n_confounders = 0L,   n_effect_modifiers = 0L,   n_instruments = 0L,   n_noise = 0L,   mc_draws = 10000L )

title: Create a causal data generating process — causalsim_dgp
text: n Positive integer. Sample size simulated dataset. effect Numeric scalar function. scalar specifies constant (homogeneous) treatment effect — ATE = CATE everywhere. function accept named arguments matching covariate names defined DGP return numeric vector individual-level causal effects (CATE). See Details. propensity Numeric scalar, preset string, function. Treatment assignment probability. scalar (e.g. 0.5) gives constant propensity (randomized trial). Preset strings "low", "moderate", "high" generate logistic propensity confounders coefficients 0.25, 0.5, 1.0 respectively. function follows named-argument convention effect must return values [0, 1]. Defaults "moderate". baseline Numeric scalar, preset string, function. Mean potential outcome control, E[Y(0) | W]. Preset strings follow levels propensity apply linear combination confounders. Defaults 0. sigma Positive numeric. Standard deviation outcome noise term. Default 1. covariates Named list covar() objects (Option / explicit path). name becomes column name generated data argument name expected effect, propensity, baseline functions. Merged auto-generated covariates; name collisions error. n_confounders Non-negative integer. Standard normal confounders auto-generated W (single) W1, W2, ... (multiple). n_effect_modifiers Non-negative integer. Auto-generates standard normal effect modifiers V V1, V2, .... n_instruments Non-negative integer. Auto-generates standard normal instruments Z Z1, Z2, .... n_noise Non-negative integer. Auto-generates standard normal noise covariates X X1, X2, .... mc_draws Positive integer. Monte Carlo draws true ATE approximation. Default 10000L. Ignored scalar effect.

title: Create a causal data generating process — causalsim_dgp
text: S3 object class causalsim_dgp components: n Sample size (integer) covar_spec Named list covar() objects effect_fn Normalized effect function propensity_fn Normalized propensity function baseline_fn Normalized baseline function sigma Outcome noise standard deviation true_ate True ATE — exact scalar, MC approximation otherwise heterogeneous Logical; TRUE effect function mc_draws Monte Carlo draws used (integer)

title: Create a causal data generating process — causalsim_dgp
text: used treatment throughout avoid collision R's built-T alias.
code: W ~ covariate_spec A ~ Bernoulli(propensity(W)) Y  = baseline(W) + effect(W) * A + N(0, sigma^2)

title: Create a causal data generating process — causalsim_dgp
text: effect, propensity, baseline functions called named arguments matching covariate names — data frame. Write:   Every argument name validated DGP's covariate spec construction time, mismatches surface immediately rather draw time. propensity function additionally evaluated small test draw confirm returns values [0, 1].
code: effect = function(W) 2 + 1.5 * W propensity = function(W1, W2) plogis(0.3 * W1 + 0.5 * W2)

title: Create a causal data generating process — causalsim_dgp
text: scalar effect true ATE exact. function effect approximated via Monte Carlo mc_draws draws covariate distribution.

title: Create a causal data generating process — causalsim_dgp
code: # Minimal: one confounder, constant effect, moderate confounding dgp <- causalsim_dgp(n = 500, n_confounders = 1, effect = 2) dgp #> <causalsim_dgp> #>   n            : 500 #>   true ATE     : 2.0000 #>   heterogeneous: FALSE #>   sigma        : 1.00 #>   covariates   : #>     W       normal  [confounder]  # Heterogeneous effect, explicit covariate spec dgp2 <- causalsim_dgp(   n = 500,   covariates = list(     W = covar("normal", role = "confounder"),     V = covar("binary", role = "effect_modifier", prob = 0.4)   ),   effect     = function(V) 2 + 1.5 * V,   propensity = function(W) plogis(0.5 * W),   baseline   = function(W) 1.5 * W )  # Mixed: shorthand confounders + explicit instrument + RCT propensity dgp3 <- causalsim_dgp(   n = 1000,   n_confounders = 2,   covariates    = list(Z = covar("normal", role = "instrument")),   effect        = 1,   propensity    = 0.5 )

what: Draw a dataset from a causal DGP — causalsim_draw
title: Draw a dataset from a causal DGP — causalsim_draw
text: Simulates one dataset causalsim_dgp() object using structural model:

title: Draw a dataset from a causal DGP — causalsim_draw
code: causalsim_draw(dgp, seed = NULL)

title: Draw a dataset from a causal DGP — causalsim_draw
text: dgp causalsim_dgp object created causalsim_dgp(). seed Integer NULL. non-null, passed set.seed() generating random values, making draw reproducible. Default NULL.

title: Draw a dataset from a causal DGP — causalsim_draw
text: data frame dgp$n rows following columns: Covariate columns One column per covariate dgp$covar_spec, named match spec (e.g. W, W1, Z). Binary treatment indicator (0/1). Y Observed outcome. .tau Individual treatment effect (CATE) — ground truth. .p Individual propensity score — ground truth. .tau .p columns carry individual-level ground truth prefixed . distinguish observed variables. causalsim_eval() uses directly without re-estimation.

title: Draw a dataset from a causal DGP — causalsim_draw
code: W ~ covariate_spec A ~ Bernoulli(propensity(W)) Y  = baseline(W) + effect(W) * A + N(0, sigma^2)

title: Draw a dataset from a causal DGP — causalsim_draw

what: Evaluate a causal estimator against a known DGP — causalsim_eval
title: Evaluate a causal estimator against a known DGP — causalsim_eval
text: Repeatedly draws datasets causalsim_dgp() object, applies user-supplied estimator draw, returns tidy summary estimator performance known ground truth.

title: Evaluate a causal estimator against a known DGP — causalsim_eval
code: causalsim_eval(   dgp,   estimator,   reps = 200L,   metrics = c("bias", "rmse", "coverage", "power"),   seed = NULL )

title: Evaluate a causal estimator against a known DGP — causalsim_eval
text: dgp causalsim_dgp object. estimator function accepts data frame produced causalsim_draw() returns named numeric vector single-row data frame. Must include estimate field. "coverage" "power" metrics, must also include ci_lower ci_upper. See Details. reps Positive integer. Number simulation replications. Default 200L. metrics Character vector. Subset "bias", "rmse", "coverage", "power". Default: four. seed Integer NULL. Passed set.seed() first replication. Default NULL.

title: Evaluate a causal estimator against a known DGP — causalsim_eval
text: S3 object class causalsim_eval components: metrics Tidy data frame: metric, value, se. draws Data frame per-replication estimates, one row per rep. true_ate DGP's true ATE. reps Number replications run.

title: Evaluate a causal estimator against a known DGP — causalsim_eval
text: estimator receives full data frame returned causalsim_draw(), including ground-truth columns .tau .p. return named numeric vector one-row data frame minimum:
code: my_estimator <- function(data) {   fit <- lm(Y ~ A + W, data = data)   est <- coef(fit)["A"]   se  <- sqrt(vcov(fit)["A", "A"])   c(estimate = est, ci_lower = est - 1.96 * se, ci_upper = est + 1.96 * se) }

title: Evaluate a causal estimator against a known DGP — causalsim_eval
text: "bias" Monte Carlo estimate bias: mean(estimate) - true_ate. "rmse" Root mean squared error: sqrt(mean((estimate - true_ate)^2)). "coverage" Proportion replications CI brackets true_ate. Requires ci_lower ci_upper. "power" Proportion replications CI excludes zero — .e., rejection rate H0: ATE = 0. true_ate != 0, power; true_ate == 0, Type error rate. Requires ci_lower ci_upper. Monte Carlo standard errors (MCSE) reported alongside metric. bias: sd(estimates) / sqrt(reps). RMSE: delta-method approximation. coverage power: Bernoulli SE.

title: Evaluate a causal estimator against a known DGP — causalsim_eval
text: Per-replication estimates stored result$draws can used custom analysis plotting.

title: Evaluate a causal estimator against a known DGP — causalsim_eval
code: dgp <- causalsim_dgp(n = 300, n_confounders = 1, effect = 2,                      propensity = 0.5)  # OLS estimator (unbiased under RCT) ols_estimator <- function(data) {   fit <- lm(Y ~ A + W, data = data)   est <- coef(fit)[["A"]]   se  <- sqrt(vcov(fit)["A", "A"])   c(estimate = est, ci_lower = est - 1.96 * se, ci_upper = est + 1.96 * se) }  result <- causalsim_eval(dgp, ols_estimator, reps = 100L, seed = 1L) result #> <causalsim_eval>  reps: 100  true ATE: 2 #>  #>    metric   value      se #>      bias -0.0061 0.01300 #>      rmse  0.1295 0.00723 #>  coverage  0.9300 0.02551 #>     power  1.0000 0.00000

what: Evaluate an estimator across a parameter grid — causalsim_grid
title: Evaluate an estimator across a parameter grid — causalsim_grid
text: Runs causalsim_eval() Cartesian product one DGP parameter values, returning tidy data frame performance metrics every cell. Designed studying estimator behavior changes sample size, confounding strength, effect size, noise level.

title: Evaluate an estimator across a parameter grid — causalsim_grid
code: causalsim_grid(   dgp,   estimator,   vary,   reps = 200L,   metrics = c("bias", "rmse", "coverage", "power"),   seed = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Evaluate an estimator across a parameter grid — causalsim_grid
text: dgp causalsim_dgp object. Provides fixed parameter values dimensions listed vary. estimator function accepted causalsim_eval(). vary Named list atomic vectors. name must valid causalsim_dgp() argument (except covariates, varied atomically). vector supplies values try dimension. full grid Cartesian product dimensions. reps Positive integer. Replications per grid cell. Default 200L. metrics Character vector. Passed causalsim_eval(). seed Integer NULL. Passed set.seed() first cell. RNG stream continues across cells, results jointly reproducible. Default NULL. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints progress message cell. Default FALSE.

title: Evaluate an estimator across a parameter grid — causalsim_grid
text: S3 object class causalsim_grid components: results Tidy data frame: one row per (cell, metric). Columns: grid parameter names, metric, value, se. grid Data frame grid cells, one row per cell. vary Character vector varied parameter names. reps Replications per cell. metrics Metrics evaluated.

title: Evaluate an estimator across a parameter grid — causalsim_grid
text: cell expand.grid(vary), causalsim_grid() takes dgp's stored original parameters, overrides cell's values, reconstructs new causalsim_dgp(), runs causalsim_eval(), tags resulting metrics cell's parameter values.

title: Evaluate an estimator across a parameter grid — causalsim_grid
text: Elements vary must atomic vectors (character, numeric, integer). Functions varied via interface. covariates (list covar() objects) excluded. complex covariate variation, construct DGPs manually use causalsim_eval() directly. Note varying n_confounders 1 2 changes auto-generated covariate names W W1, W2. base DGP's effect function references W, reconstructed DGP error construction time. Design base DGP accordingly.

title: Evaluate an estimator across a parameter grid — causalsim_grid
code: dgp <- causalsim_dgp(n = 500, n_confounders = 1, effect = 2,                      propensity = 0.5)  ols_estimator <- function(data) {   fit <- lm(Y ~ A + W, data = data)   est <- coef(fit)[["A"]]   se  <- sqrt(vcov(fit)["A", "A"])   c(estimate = est, ci_lower = est - 1.96 * se, ci_upper = est + 1.96 * se) }  grid_result <- causalsim_grid(   dgp       = dgp,   estimator = ols_estimator,   vary      = list(n = c(250L, 500L, 1000L)),   reps      = 50L,   seed      = 1L ) grid_result #> <causalsim_grid>  3 cells  vary: n  reps/cell: 50 #>   metrics: bias, rmse, coverage, power #>  #>     n   metric        value          se #>   250     bias -0.018135254 0.019081996 #>   250     rmse  0.134799456 0.011981623 #>   250 coverage  0.900000000 0.042426407 #>   250    power  1.000000000 0.000000000 #>   500     bias -0.007350673 0.012609789 #>   500     rmse  0.088574059 0.009552722 #>   500 coverage  0.940000000 0.033585711 #>   500    power  1.000000000 0.000000000 #>  1000     bias  0.010538141 0.010225101 #>  1000     rmse  0.072347315 0.006066306 #>  #>   ... 2 more rows. Access full results via $results.

what: Define a covariate for a causal DGP — covar
title: Define a covariate for a causal DGP — covar
text: Constructs fully-specified covariate descriptor use causalsim_dgp(). Every covariate distribution family, optional distribution parameters, one causal roles control enters automatically-generated treatment outcome models.

title: Define a covariate for a causal DGP — covar
code: covar(dist = "normal", role = "confounder", ...)

title: Define a covariate for a causal DGP — covar
text: dist Character. Distribution family. One "normal", "binary", "uniform". role Character vector. Causal role(s). One : "confounder" Enters propensity model outcome baseline — creates confounding bias naive estimators. "instrument" Enters propensity model (exclusion restriction holds). "effect_modifier" Available use effect function; excluded propensity model unless also "confounder". "noise" Independent treatment outcome; adds variance without confounding. Roles mutually exclusive: role = c("confounder", "effect_modifier") specifies variable confounds moderates effect. ... Distribution parameters. "normal": mean (default 0), sd (default 1) "binary": prob (default 0.5) "uniform": min (default 0), max (default 1)

title: Define a covariate for a causal DGP — covar
text: S3 object class causalsim_covar.

title: Define a covariate for a causal DGP — covar
code: # Standard normal confounder covar("normal", role = "confounder", mean = 0, sd = 1) #> <causalsim_covar>  dist: normal(mean=0, sd=1)  role: [confounder]  # Binary effect modifier covar("binary", role = "effect_modifier", prob = 0.4) #> <causalsim_covar>  dist: binary(prob=0.4)      role: [effect_modifier]  # Variable that both confounds and moderates the effect covar("normal", role = c("confounder", "effect_modifier")) #> <causalsim_covar>  dist: normal                role: [confounder, effect_modifier]

title: Plot the distribution of estimates from a causalsim_eval result — plot.causalsim_eval
text: x causalsim_eval object. ... Additional arguments passed graphics::hist().

title: Plot the distribution of estimates from a causalsim_eval result — plot.causalsim_eval

title: Summarise a causalsim_eval result — summary.causalsim_eval
text: Returns structured summary estimator performance including metrics table distribution per-replication estimates (mean, SD, median, 10th / 90th percentiles).

title: Summarise a causalsim_eval result — summary.causalsim_eval
code: # S3 method for class 'causalsim_eval' summary(object, ...)

title: Summarise a causalsim_eval result — summary.causalsim_eval
text: S3 object class causalsim_eval_summary components: metrics metrics data frame original result. mean_estimate Mean per-replication estimates. sd_estimate Standard deviation per-replication estimates. median_estimate Median per-replication estimates. p10_estimate 10th percentile per-replication estimates. p90_estimate 90th percentile per-replication estimates. true_ate True ATE DGP. reps Number replications.
